title: Clompanus propinqua
uses: Clompanus propinqua is a plant species native to New South Wales in Australia. Unfortunately, there is limited information available about its medicinal uses. However, it is often studied by botanists and researchers due to its unique properties and potential benefits. Further research is needed to understand its full potential for medicinal applications. More information on its uses would require further investigation and analysis.

parts: Clompanus propinqua, also known as the Tasmanian blueberry, is a shrub native to Tasmania. The medicinal parts of the plant are not well-documented, but the leaves and berries may have potential uses in traditional medicine. In some studies, the leaves of Clompanus propinqua have shown antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of this plant. preparations: Clompanus propinqua is a plant species native to the Solomon Islands, and various herbal preparations have been traditionally used by the local communities. The plant's leaves have been used in infusions to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. The leaves are often boiled in water to create a decoction, which is then consumed to reap the perceived health benefits.
